metadata.name: ml4cps
metadata.summary: Tools for learning, plotting, analyzing etc. of discrete, continuous, timed, and hybrid cyber-physical systems.

modules.name: ml4cps.decisions
documentation:
The module provides base classes to represent the dynamics of cyber-physical systems (CPS): CPS and
CPSComponent.

Author: Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de

modules.name: ml4cps.learn
documentation:
The module provides learning algorithms for creation of different kinds of automata.

Authors:
- Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de, nemanja@ai4cps.com
- Tom Westermann, tom.westermann@hsu-hh.de, tom@ai4cps.com

functions.name: ml4cps.learn.simple_learn_from_event_logs
documentation: Simple algorithm to learn a timed automaton.
norm_coeff = np.array([[2.0], [3.0], [1.0]])  # Normalization factors for both xout and udout
xout, udout, xout_shifts = FnShiftAndDiff(xout, udout, norm_coeff, num_var, num_ud, max_deriv, Ts)

functions.name: ml4cps.learn.build_pta
documentation:
In the function build_pta the prefix tree acceptor is created by going through each sequence of events in the
learning examples. Also, the depth, in- and out-degree of the states are set.

functions.name: ml4cps.learn.extend_states
documentation:
The function extend_states takes the current alphabet, which was created in the beginning and extends it via taking
the minima of the pdfs and splitting the events, if there are at least three minima.

functions.name: ml4cps.tools.remove_timestamps_without_change
documentation: Removes timestamps where no values changed in comparison to the previous timestamp.

functions.name: ml4cps.tools.binary_ordinal_encode
documentation:
Encodes a pandas Series with binary ordinal encoding based on the specified order.

Args:
    column (pd.Series): The column to encode.
    order (list): The ordered list of unique values in the column.

Returns:
    pd.DataFrame: The binary ordinal encoded DataFrame for the given column.

functions.name: ml4cps.tools.generate_random_walk
documentation:
Generates a random walk process for multiple variables.

Parameters:
- start_values (list): A list of starting values for each variable.
- steps (int): Number of steps in the random walk.

Returns:
- pd.DataFrame: DataFrame containing the random walk process for each variable.

modules.name: ml4cps.sim
documentation:
Simulation.

Author:
- Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de, nemanja@ai4cps.com

methods.name: ml4cps.sim.Simulator.stop_condition
documentation: Simulation stop condition which can be overridden by the subclasses.

modules.name: ml4cps.examples
documentation:
Author: Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de

Description: The module provides example systems and datasets.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.detect_change_points
documentation:
Detects local and global changepoints in the input and output traces.
A sliding window approach utilizing the Euclidean distance between the
immediate past and immediate future is used to detect changes in dynamic
behavior on all available derivatives.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.compute_past_future_diff_distance
documentation:
Returns the similarity of the immediate past to the immediate future for all inner indices
using the Euclidean distance metric.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.find_changepoints
documentation: Recursively finds changepoints present in the interval using all available derivatives up to the selected one.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.filter_change_points
documentation:
Applies filtering to global and local changepoints to remove close proximity changepoints
and ensures consistency across global and local changepoint sets.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.filter_consecutive_changepoints
documentation: Removes the second changepoint if two changepoints are within a specified window.

functions.name: ml4cps.famos.build_decision_tree
documentation:
FnBuildDT trains a Decision Tree using feature vectors provided by X and classifiers provided by Y.

Parameters:
X (array-like): Feature matrix.
Y (array-like): Labels/classifiers.

Returns:
Mdl (DecisionTreeClassifier): Trained Decision Tree model.
impure_leaves (int): Number of impure leaves.
num_nodes (int): Total number of nodes in the tree.
learn_time (float): Time taken to train the model.

modules.name: ml4cps.reconfig
documentation:
Author: Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de

Description: The module provides models and algorithms for the reconfiguration of CPSs.

functions.name: ml4cps.examples.examples.buck_converter
documentation:
Credits to the FAMOS paper authors.

:return:

functions.name: ml4cps.examples.examples.simple_conveyor
documentation:
We model the discrete-event controller with a three-state automaton.
The idle conveyor is in state $q_{idle}$ until an item with a mass $M$ and a destination distance $D$ is put on it.
Then it switches to $q_{move}$ during which the conveyor is moving the item.
It is in this state until the destination position is reached, and it switches to $q_{settle}$.
After $T_{settle}$ amount of time it is again in $q_{idle}$.
:return:

modules.name: ml4cps.reconfig.reconfig
documentation: CPS reconfiguration is a process of finding a configuration that will bring/maintain the system in the desired state.

modules.name: ml4cps.denta
documentation:
The module implements the novel DENTA algorithm for the learning of hybrid automata from data.

Author:
Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de, nemanja@ai4cps.com

methods.name: ml4cps.denta.DENTA.dsm_loss
documentation:
DSM loss from
A Connection Between Score Matching
    and Denoising Autoencoders
The loss is computed as
x_ = x + v   # noisy samples
s = -dE(x_)/dx_
loss = 1/2*||s + (x-x_)/sigma^2||^2
Args:
    x (torch.Tensor): input samples
    v (torch.Tensor): sampled noises
    sigma (float, optional): noise scale. Defaults to 0.1.

Returns:
    DSM loss

methods.name: ml4cps.denta.DENTA.gmm_model
documentation: Transforms the p(v) into mixture of Gaussians and returns the weight, mean and sigma for each Gaussian component as
well the corresponding hidden states.This function is for use with very small models.Otherwize it will last forever

methods.name: ml4cps.denta.DENTA.compute_purity
documentation:
Computes the purity between cluster and class assignments.
Compare to https://nlp.stanford.edu/IR-book/html/htmledition/evaluation-of-clustering-1.html

Args:
    cluster_assignments (list): List of cluster assignments for every point.
    class_assignments (list): List of class assignments for every point.

Returns:
    float: The purity value.

modules.name: ml4cps.reconfig.autoconf
documentation:
AutoConf algorithm was proposed in [1].

[1] Balzereit, K., & Niggemann, O. (2022).
AutoConf: A New Algorithm for Reconfiguration of Cyber-Physical Production Systems.
IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics. https://doi.org/10.1109/TII.2022.3146940

functions.name: ml4cps.reconfig.autoconf.autoconf
documentation: AutoConf needs a causal model in form of a bi-adjacency matrix.

modules.name: ml4cps.discretization.discretization

The module provides base classes to represent the dynamics of cyber-physical systems (CPS): CPS and
CPSComponent.

Author: Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de

classes.name: ml4cps.discretization.discretization.TimeSeriesDiscretizer
documentation: Abstract class that encapsulates methods used for the discretization of time series.

classes.name: ml4cps.discretization.discretization.EqualWidthDiscretizer

methods.name: ml4cps.discretization.discretization.EqualWidthDiscretizer.train
documentation:
Estimate model parameters, thresholds that divide each variable into equal-width intervals.
\:param data: Data to calculate model parameters from.
\:param number_of_intervals: Number of equal-width intervals per variable.
\:return:

methods.name: ml4cps.discretization.discretization.EqualWidthDiscretizer.discretize
documentation:
Discretize data into equal width intervals.
\:param data: Data to discretize.
\:return: Discretized data.

A class that implements equal-width interval (EWI) discretization method. It calculates range of every variable
(column) of the input data into a predefined number of equal-width intervals.

modules.name: ml4cps.vis
documentation:
The module provides methods to visualize various kinds of data, such as time series or automata graphs.

Authors:
- Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de, nemanja@ai4cps.com
- Tom Westermann, tom.westermann@hsu-hh.de, tom@ai4cps.com

functions.name: ml4cps.vis.plot_timeseries
documentation:
Using plotly library, plots each variable (column) in a collection of dataframe as subplots, one after another.

Arguments:
plotStates (bool): if True, changes interpolation to 'hv', therefore keeping the last value
yAxisLabelOffset (bool): if True, adds an offset to the plots y-axis labels. Improves readability on long subplot names.

Returns:
    fig (plotly.Figure):

functions.name: ml4cps.vis.plot_cps_component
documentation:
---
param cps: true
param edge_font_size: true
param edge_labels: true
param edge_text_max_width: true
param id: true
param node_labels: true
param output: true
return: true
---

functions.name: ml4cps.vis.plot_cps
documentation:
Plots all the components of a CPS in the same figure.
\:param cps: CPS to plot.
\:param node_labels: Should node labels be plotted.
\:param edge_labels: Should edge labels be plotted.
\:param node_size: What is the size of the nodes in the figure.
\:param node_font_size: The font size of the node labels.
\:param edge_font_size: The font size of the edge labels.
\:param edge_text_max_width: Max width of the edge labels.
\:param output: Should output be plotted as a dash.Cytoscape component ("cyto"), or should dash server be run
("dash").
\:param dash_port: If temporary dash server is run, what port to use.
\:param kwargs: Other paramters are forwarded to the Cytoscape component.
\:return:

modules.name: ml4cps.cps
documentation:
The module provides base classes to represent the dynamics of cyber-physical systems (CPS):
- CPS, and,
- CPSComponent.

Author: Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de

classes.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.__init__
documentation:
Initializes CPS with the given attributes.

Args:
    sys_id (str): ID of the system.
    components (iterable): Child components of the system.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.parent_system
documentation:
Gets the parent system by accessing _parent_system private attribute.

Returns:
    (CPS): The parent system.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.parent_system
documentation:
Sets the _parent_system of this component and adds the child of the parent system.

Args:
    parent_system (CPS): The new parent system.

Raises:
    AttributeError: If we cannot set child of the parent system or if the component with the same id is already
    a child of the parent system.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.__getitem__
documentation:
Gets the component with the given key in the collection.

Args:
    key (string): The key where the value will be stored. Must be a hashable type (e.g., int, str).
Return:
    (CPS, CPSComponent): Returned component or subsystem.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.items
documentation: Method to get components as in dict.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.get_components
documentation:
Returns a list of all direct child components except those with ids possibly provided in exclude.

Parameters
----------
exclude : iterable
    A list of component ids to exclude in the returned list (default is None).

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.set_child_component
documentation:
Set component with the id.

Parameters
----------
id : str
    ID of the component to add.
com : (CPS, CPSComponent)
    Component of subsystem to add.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.get_all_components
documentation:
Returns a list of all child components (not only direct) except those with ids possibly provided in exclude.

Parameters
----------
exclude : iterable
    A list of component ids to exclude in the returned list.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.reinitialize
documentation:
The function re-initializes the CPS components of this CPS with the given state values.
\:param t: Current time to set to the components.
\:param state: State of the CPS (it's components) given as a dictionary of dictionaries ... of tuples (according
to the CPS hierarchy). The tuplus are created as values concatenated values of discrete-event state variables,
time-continuous state variables and time-discrete state variables.

```
return

```

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPS.simulate
documentation:
Simulates behaviour of the system until the finish_time is reached.

Parameters
----------
finish_time : Time when simulation finishes.
verbose : Should the execution log be printed in detail (default is False).
save_choices : Should the choices for each component be saved to json files after
the simulation is finished (default is False).

CPS class represents a cyber-physical system. It is used to model the hierarchy in the system as each CPS can
contain a mixture of other CPS and CPS components. The leaves of the hierarchy are only CPS component objects
which define their dynamics and communication with other components.

Attributes:
    id (str): Identifier of the object.
    com (OrderedDict): Property. A collection of components.
    parent_system (CPS): Parent CPS that this (sub)system belongs to.

classes.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent

System state is a vector of all state variables.
:return:

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent.simulation_process_simpy
documentation:
The function simulates single concurrent thread of one component (CPSComponent).
Parameters
----------
env : simpy.Environment
    It is simpy environment used to synchronize multiple parallel threads during simulation.
max_time : float
    It is the maximum time that the simulation will perform.
verbose : bool
    Should the function print execution logs (default is False).

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent.simulate
documentation:
Simulates behaviour of the system until the finish_time is reached.

Parameters
----------
finish_time : Time when simulation finishes.
verbose : Should the execution log be printed in detail (default is False).
save_choices : Should the choices for each component be saved to json files after

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent.__step_continuous
documentation:
Simulates one time step of continuous behavior from t to t+dt. Underlying function is solve_ivp with method is 'RK23'.
: ```
  param x0

  Initial state vector.

  param t

  Time at start of the step simulation.

  param u

  Arguments passed.....

  return

  Time t+dt, value of state at t+dt
  ```

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent.timed_event
documentation:
Calculates if and when the next time event will happen and the new state values.
\:param t: Current time.
\:param q: Discrete-event part of the state.
\:param xc: Time-continuous part of the state.
\:param xd: Time-discrete part of the state.
\:return: Time delay of the timed event, new state value.

methods.name: ml4cps.cps.CPSComponent.apply_sim_event
documentation:
The event e is applied in this component's simpy execution, this means that the process must wait for an event.
\:param e: Event to apply.
\:return:
General hybrid system class based on scipy and simpy.

modules.name: ml4cps.automata
documentation:
The module provides a class Automaton which inherits CPSComponent and implements the dynamics of different kinds of
automata.

Authors:
- Nemanja Hranisavljevic, hranisan@hsu-hh.de, nemanja@ai4cps.com
- Tom Westermann, tom.westermann@hsu-hh.de, tom@ai4cps.com

classes.name: ml4cps.automata.Automaton

methods.name: ml4cps.automata.Automaton.__init__
documentation:
Class initialization from lists of elements.
\:param states: Discrete states / modes of continuous behavior.
\:param events: The events that trigger state transitions.
\:param transitions: The transition information. If a collection of dicts then dict should contain "source",
"dest" and "event". The other attributes will be added as data of that transition. Alternatively, a collection
of tuples can be used of the form (source, event, dest, ).
\:param unknown_state: The name of unknown states during "play in", if "raise", an exception will be raised.

Returns the number of modes in the automaton.

documentation: 'Initial state not defined, sampling initial state uniformly from the set of all states.')
Automaton class is the main class for modeling various kinds of hybrid systems.
